summary: Split Kirby's copied Jigglypuff/Purin SpecialN code out of `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` into a new linked matching object, `ftkirbyspecialpurin.c`, with its own header. The PR adjusted GALE01 split ranges and symbol ownership so the Purin block at 0x80100E0C-0x80105A34 links as `Object(Matching)`, while trimming the Donkey file back to its own range. It also moved adjacent Zelda-special declarations/code into a new `ftkirbyspecialzelda.h`, relocated the shared `SIGNF` macro to `src/MSL/math.h`, and cleaned up function forward declarations/static linkage around the split boundaries.

key_files.role: new matching source file for Kirby Purin/Jigglypuff SpecialN code previously embedded after the Donkey-special block
new public header containing address-commented declarations for Purin SpecialN callbacks
source trimmed to end at the Donkey section; removed roughly 1400 lines of Purin/Zelda-adjacent code
removed Purin and Zelda declarations that no longer belonged in the Donkey header
received adjacent Zelda helper definitions and included its new header
new header for Zelda copy-special declarations
split ranges updated: Donkey shortened, Purin object inserted, Zelda text start moved to 0x80105A34
symbol scope/names updated for data, sdata, and sdata2 belonging to the new split
removed duplicate local `SIGNF` macro after moving it to MSL math

what_changed: Inserted a new split entry for `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialpurin.c` covering `.text` 0x80100E0C-0x80105A34, `.data` 0x803CB710-0x803CB770, `.sdata` 0x804D3DD0-0x804D3DE8, and `.sdata2` 0x804D9498-0x804D94F0.

Moved the Purin SpecialN callback family into `ftkirbyspecialpurin.c`, including animation, IASA, physics, collision, hit, turn, start/loop/full/end, and air variants such as `ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftKb_PrSpecialN1_Phys`, and `ftKb_PrSpecialNHit_Coll`.
Added `ftkirbyspecialpurin.h` with address-commented declarations beginning at 0x80100F94 and ending at 0x8010581C.
Removed Purin and Zelda declarations from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, keeping Donkey ownership cleaner.

Added `ftkirbyspecialzelda.h` and included it from `ftKb_Init.c`/`ftkirbyspecialzelda.c`; Zelda declarations such as `ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C` and `ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Coll` now live outside the Donkey header.
Moved Zelda helper definitions `fn_80105A34` and `fn_80105AB0` into `ftkirbyspecialzelda.c` near the adjusted boundary.

Converted several symbol-table entries around the new Purin data ranges from generic `ftKb_Init_*` globals to local labels such as `@244`, `@245`, `@246`, and sdata2 locals `@247`, `@248`, `@264`, etc.
Changed `ftKb_Init_803CB758` from a `Vec3` array in `ftKb_Init.c` to an `ftCollisionBox` object defined in `ftkirbyspecialpurin.c`.
Added `SIGNF(x)` to `src/MSL/math.h` and removed local duplicate definitions from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` and `ftPr_SpecialN.c`.
Registered `ftkirbyspecialpurin.c` as a matching object in `configure.py`.
smart_moves: The split boundary was chosen at exact function/object boundaries: Donkey ends at 0x80100E0C, Purin starts at 0x80100E0C, and Zelda starts at 0x80105A34 after moving the adjacent Zelda helpers.
Rather than leaving declarations for copied-character specials in `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, the PR introduced focused Purin and Zelda headers, making future split ownership easier to reason about.
Data ownership was corrected alongside text ownership; the PR did not only split `.text`, but also moved `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` ranges and symbol scopes to match the new object.
The local scale tables `ftKb_Init_803CB710` and `ftKb_Init_803CB720` became static inside the new Purin file, reducing false global coupling.
The collision-box data was typed as `ftCollisionBox` in the owning Purin file, matching call sites that pass `&ftKb_Init_803CB758` to collision helpers.
Hoisting `SIGNF` into `MSL/math.h` avoided repeated local macro definitions in both Kirby Purin-copy code and real Purin SpecialN code.
decomp_lessons: When splitting copied-character Kirby code, do not trust the old aggregate file name; inspect exact address ranges and move adjacent character-specific helpers to their true neighboring object if the split boundary requires it.
A matching split usually needs coordinated updates in `splits.txt`, `symbols.txt`, `configure.py`, source files, and headers; changing only the C file is insufficient.
After a source split, revisit symbol visibility. Constants that were previously forced global as `ftKb_Init_*` may become compiler-generated local labels once their owning object is isolated.

Header cleanup matters for decomp maintainability: copied-special declarations should live in `ftkirbyspecial<character>.h`, not in whichever earlier file happened to contain the code before splitting.
Shared utility macros found duplicated during a split should be centralized if evidence shows multiple users; here `SIGNF(x)` was moved to `MSL/math.h` and duplicates were removed.

Data typing can unblock clean splits: the `ftKb_Init_803CB758` data is used as an `ftCollisionBox`, so defining it that way in the owning file is preferable to leaving it as an unrelated vector array in init code.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special files follow lowercase `ftkirbyspecial<source-character>.c/.h`, e.g. `ftkirbyspecialpurin` and `ftkirbyspecialzelda`.
Kirby Purin-copy functions use `ftKb_PrSpecial...` and `ftKb_SpecialNPr_...`; `Pr` distinguishes Purin/Jigglypuff copy-specific code.
Zelda-copy functions use `ftKb_ZdSpecial...` and `ftKb_SpecialNZd_...`; `Zd` distinguishes Zelda copy-specific code.
Address comments are preserved in declarations and local forward declarations, e.g. `/* 1016CC */ void ftKb_PrSpecialNStart_Anim(...)` and `/* 105A34 */ static void fn_80105A34(...)`.
Compiler-local data labels in `symbols.txt` use `@NNN` names when the data is local to the split object; forced global `ftKb_Init_<addr>` names are retained only where still needed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `splits.txt` to carve the new object across all relevant sections, not only `.text`; this PR added Purin `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` ranges in addition to text.
Promote the new C file in `configure.py` as `Object(Matching, ...)` only after the isolated range links correctly.

When splitting an object, check the immediate next function after the desired range. Here the Zelda text start shifted from 0x80105B2C to 0x80105A34 because `fn_80105A34`/`fn_80105AB0` belonged with Zelda after Purin ended at 0x80105A34.
Use static local arrays for file-owned constants to influence data symbol locality and ordering, as with `ftKb_Init_803CB710` and `ftKb_Init_803CB720`.

Global-to-local symbol changes in `symbols.txt` can be a sign that the split now matches compiler ownership; examples include Purin sdata2 constants changing from `ftKb_Init_804D9498` style names to `@247`-style locals.
The diff evidence shows no reviewer discussion, so no human rationale beyond the mechanical split/link evidence can be confirmed.

review_feedback.note: The PR context reports zero issue comments, zero review comments, and zero reviews; no explicit reviewer feedback was available.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftKb_PrSpecialN*` functions from address-based names to semantic names.
Find subsequent splits of Kirby copied-special files that reuse the `ftkirbyspecial<character>.h` header pattern.
Check whether `SIGNF(x)` in `MSL/math.h` remains used by both Kirby Purin copy code and `ftPr_SpecialN.c`.
Look for future cleanup of `ftKb_Init_803CB758` and other `ftKb_Init_*` data names after this split.
Search for later matching work on `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.c` now that its range was shortened.
